overrides.override_summary: Must comply with CA fast food labor laws including CFFC authority to increase minimum wage (AB 1228)
overrides.why_it_matters: Higher labor costs for CA franchisees; minimum wage may be set above state/federal minimums by CFFC

overrides.override_summary: Item 17(v) choice of forum deleted for FA and ADA
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees may not be forced to litigate in NJ

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs FA and ADA (except federal arbitration and federal law)
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise law protections apply instead of NJ law

overrides.override_summary: Section 41 of IL Franchise Disclosure Act: any waiver of IL compliance is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive IL franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: Termination and non-renewal rights governed by Sections 19 and 20 of IL FDA
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional protections on termination/non-renewal for IL franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Maryland Securities Commissioner required surety bond from franchisor
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional financial protection for MD franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Company will protect franchisee's trademark rights and indemnify from trademark claims per Minn. Stat. §80C.12 Subd. 1(g)
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee has trademark defense protection not available in base FA

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages provision will not be enforced to extent prohibited by MN law
overrides.why_it_matters: Potential reduction in post-termination financial exposure for MN franchisees

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (60 days to cure); 180 days notice for non-renewal per Minn. Stat. §80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer cure/notice periods than base FA (which provides 10-30 days)

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside MN; cannot require waiver of jury trial; cannot require liquidated damages/termination penalties/judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees can litigate in MN, retain jury trial right, and avoid liquidated damages

overrides.override_summary: Release as condition of renewal/transfer does not apply to claims under Minn. Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees retain franchise law claims at renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: Forum selection provisions restricted under ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees may litigate in ND

overrides.override_summary: ND statutes of limitations apply to claims under ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND limitations apply, not NJ

overrides.override_summary: Venue and applicable law modified for RI franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law protections apply

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in any conflict of laws
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchise law supersedes NJ law provisions

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed location; litigation may be brought in WA
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees avoid mandatory NJ venue

overrides.override_summary: Release/waiver of WA franchise law rights is void except via negotiated settlement with independent counsel per RCW 19.100.220(2)
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot be forced to waive WA franchise law claims at renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: Provisions unreasonably restricting WA Act limitations or jury trial rights may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves WA franchisee remedies

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees must reflect reasonable estimate or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: May limit $15K flat transfer fee if not cost-justified

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate under WA state law grounds; buy-back provisions limited to good cause termination only
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional termination rights and buy-back protection for WA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Requiring purchase/rent of products at more than fair and reasonable price is unlawful under RCW 19.100.180(2)(d)
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees may challenge above-market supplier pricing
